title: Stigmatodactylus corniculatus
taxonomy: Stigmatodactylus corniculatus is a species of epiphytic orchid. It belongs to the family Vandeae, which is a subtribe within the orchid family Orchidaceae. The Vandeae subtribe is further divided into several genera, with Stigmatodactylus being one of them. The classification of Stigmatodactylus corniculatus is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Monocots, Order: Asparagales, Family: Orchidaceae, Subtribe: Vandeae, Genus: Stigmatodactylus. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and position within the plant kingdom.
uses: Stigmatodactylus corniculatus, a species of leafy liverwort, has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine. intro: Stigmatodactylus corniculatus, also known as the Corniculated Stigmatodactyl, is a type of epiphytic orchid native to tropical regions of Australia. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Stigmatodactylus corniculatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stigmatodactylus corniculatus is often prepared as a tea or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
